SEARCH
Package org.openapitools.codegen.config

Class GeneratorSettings

java.lang.Object
org.openapitools.codegen.config.GeneratorSettings
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class GeneratorSettings extends Object implements Serializable
Represents those settings applied to a generator.
See Also:

  • Method Details

    • getGeneratorName

      public String getGeneratorName()
      Gets the name of the generator to use.
      Returns:
      the generator name

    • getApiPackage

      public String getApiPackage()
      Gets the api package name for generated sources.
      Returns:
      the api package

    • getModelPackage

      public String getModelPackage()
      Gets the model package name for generated sources
      Returns:
      the model package

    • getInvokerPackage

      public String getInvokerPackage()
      Gets the invoker package name for generated sources.
      Returns:
      the invoker package

    • getPackageName

      public String getPackageName()
      Gets the overall package name for generated sources.
      Returns:
      the package name

    • getApiNameSuffix

      public String getApiNameSuffix()
      Gets a api name suffix for generated models. This name will be appended to a api name.

      This option is often used to circumvent compilation issues where models match keywords.

      Example:

      Suffix Gen applied to Object results in a generated class named ObjectGen.

      Returns:
      the model name suffix

    • getModelNamePrefix

      public String getModelNamePrefix()
      Gets a model name prefix for generated models. This name will be prefixed to a model name.

      This option is often used to circumvent compilation issues where models match keywords.

      Example:

      Prefix My applied to Object results in a generated class named MyObject.

      Returns:
      the model name prefix

    • getModelNameSuffix

      public String getModelNameSuffix()
      Gets a model name suffix for generated models. This name will be appended to a model name.

      This option is often used to circumvent compilation issues where models match keywords.

      Example:

      Suffix Gen applied to Object results in a generated class named ObjectGen.

      Returns:
      the model name suffix

    • getGroupId

      public String getGroupId()
      Gets the group id for generated sources which support this concept (e.g. Java and pom.xml, Scala and SBT/Gradle/pom).
      Returns:
      the group id

    • getArtifactId

      public String getArtifactId()
      Gets artifact id for generated sources which support this concept (e.g. Java and pom.xml, Scala and SBT/Gradle/pom).
      Returns:
      the artifact id

    • getArtifactVersion

      public String getArtifactVersion()
      Gets artifact version for generated sources which support this concept (e.g. Java and pom.xml, Scala and SBT/Gradle/pom).
      Returns:
      the artifact version

    • getLibrary

      public String getLibrary()
      Gets library (sub-template) for the target generated.
      Returns:
      the library

    • getInstantiationTypes

      public Map<String,String> getInstantiationTypes()
      Gets instantiation types mappings. These allow for customizing the defaults provided by a built-in generator.

      For example, "array" to "ArrayList" applied to the Java generator will cause all array properties to be instantiated as ArrayList.

      This option differs from getTypeMappings() in that values provided here are generally used for type construction (what is applied to "new").

      Returns:
      the instantiation types

    • getTypeMappings

      public Map<String,String> getTypeMappings()
      Gets type mappings. These allow for customizing type definitions.

      For example, "array" to "List" applied to the Java generator will cause all variable assignments for array properties to be of type List.

      This option differs from getInstantiationTypes() in that values provided here are variable reference types rather than concrete instantiation types.

      Returns:
      the type mappings

    • getAdditionalProperties

      public Map<String,Object> getAdditionalProperties()
      Gets additional properties which will be passed to template as dynamic properties.
      Returns:
      the additional properties

    • getImportMappings

      public Map<String,String> getImportMappings()
      Gets import mappings between a given class and the import that should be used for that class.

      Use import mappings, for example, when you want to "bring your own models" from another location.

      Returns:
      the import mappings
      See Also:
      • Bringing your own models

    • getSchemaMappings

      public Map<String,String> getSchemaMappings()
      Gets schema mappings between a schema and the new name.
      Returns:
      the schema mappings

    • getInlineSchemaNameMappings

      public Map<String,String> getInlineSchemaNameMappings()
      Gets inline schema name mappings between an inline schema name and the new name.
      Returns:
      the inline schema name mappings

    • getInlineSchemaOptions

      public Map<String,String> getInlineSchemaOptions()
      Gets inline schema options
      Returns:
      the inline schema options

    • getNameMappings

      public Map<String,String> getNameMappings()
      Gets property name mappings between a property name and the new name.
      Returns:
      the property name mappings

    • getParameterNameMappings

      public Map<String,String> getParameterNameMappings()
      Gets parameter name mappings between a parameter name and the new name.
      Returns:
      the parameter name mappings

    • getModelNameMappings

      public Map<String,String> getModelNameMappings()
      Gets model name mappings between a model name and the new name.
      Returns:
      the model name mappings

    • getEnumNameMappings

      public Map<String,String> getEnumNameMappings()
      Gets enum name mappings between an enum name and the new name.
      Returns:
      the enum name mappings

    • getOperationIdNameMappings

      public Map<String,String> getOperationIdNameMappings()
      Gets operation id name mappings between an operation id name and the new name.
      Returns:
      the operation id name mappings

    • getOpenapiNormalizer

      public Map<String,String> getOpenapiNormalizer()
      Gets OpenAPI normalizer rules
      Returns:
      a map of rules

    • getLanguageSpecificPrimitives

      public Set<String> getLanguageSpecificPrimitives()
      Gets language specific primitives. These are in addition to the "base" primitives defined in a generator.

      In general, a primitive defined here will indicate to the generator:

      - models with these types don't require an import - model names not included here require imports and likely indicate a model reference

      There may be generator-specific implementation details which differ slightly.

      Returns:
      the language specific primitives

    • getOpenapiGeneratorIgnoreList

      public Set<String> getOpenapiGeneratorIgnoreList()
      Gets openapi generator ignore list.
      Returns:
      the openapi generator ignore list

    • getReservedWordsMappings

      public Map<String,String> getReservedWordsMappings()
      Gets reserved word mappings. Values defined here define how a reserved word should be escaped.

      If no mapping is present, the mapping is generally automatically applied to a default with prefixed underscore (_name). Note that some languages don't support identifiers beginning with a prefix, in which case the generator applies a more appropriate prefix.

      Returns:
      the reserved word mappings

    • getServerVariables

      public Map<String,String> getServerVariables()
      Gets server variable. Values defined here will be attempted to be replaced within a templated server object.
      Returns:
      the server variables

    • getGitHost

      public String getGitHost()
      Gets git host. e.g. gitlab.com.

      Generally used by git_push.sh in generated sources which support it. This value may also be used by templates in maven style references, READMEs, or other documentation.

      Returns:
      the git host

    • getGitUserId

      public String getGitUserId()
      Gets git user id. e.g. openapitools.

      Generally used by git_push.sh in generated sources which support it. This value may also be used by templates in maven style references, READMEs, or other documentation.

      Returns:
      the git user id

    • getGitRepoId

      public String getGitRepoId()
      Gets git repo id. e.g. openapi-generator.

      Generally used by git_push.sh in generated sources which support it. This value may also be used by templates in maven style references, READMEs, or other documentation.

      Returns:
      the git repo id

    • getReleaseNote

      public String getReleaseNote()
      Gets release note for the generated instance.

      Generally used by git_push.sh in generated sources which support it. This value may also be used by templates in maven style references, READMEs, or other documentation.

      Returns:
      the release note

    • getHttpUserAgent

      public String getHttpUserAgent()
      Gets the http user agent to be used by client generators which support setting this value.

      e.g. codegen_csharp_api_client, defaults to 'OpenAPI-Generator/{packageVersion}/{language}'

      Returns:
      the http user agent

    • newBuilder

      public static GeneratorSettings.Builder newBuilder()
      New builder builder.
      Returns:
      the builder

    • newBuilder

      public static GeneratorSettings.Builder newBuilder(GeneratorSettings copy)
